findings.description: parse() does list(reader) to materialize the whole CSV, then builds a second full list of Record objects, holding two complete copies of the dataset in memory (O(n) blowup / OOM risk on large uploads); iterate the reader in a single pass instead.

stream_valid() advertises streaming via its name and Iterator return type but calls self.parse(raw), eagerly materializing the entire file into a list[Record] before the first yield, so peak memory is O(n) regardless of how few records the caller consumes; parse incrementally inside the generator.
count_rows() uses open(path).readlines() to load the entire file into a discarded list just to count lines (O(file-size) memory where O(1) suffices); use a with-block and sum(1 for _ in f) - 1.

parse() builds a name->index map (index) but never uses it, instead hardcoding positional row[0..3], so a column-order mismatch silently corrupts data and a short row raises IndexError — defer detail to critic-logic.
